message:
fix(ubuntu_upgrade): handle point release versions correctly

Fix ubuntu_get_version_number() to correctly parse Ubuntu point release
versions like "24.04.1" and extract just the major.minor components:

Before:
- "24.04" -> "2404" (correct)
- "24.04.1" -> "2404.1" or parse error (incorrect)
- "25.10" -> "2510" (correct)

After:
- "24.04" -> "2404" (correct)
- "24.04.1" -> "2404" (now correct)
- "25.10" -> "2510" (correct)

The fix extracts the remainder after the first dot, then takes only
the portion before any subsequent dots to get the minor version number.
This matches how Ubuntu versioning works - point releases (24.04.1,
24.04.2) are patch updates to the base release and should compare as
equal to the base version for upgrade path calculations.

Updated the function comment to document the new behavior with the
point release example.

This prevents upgrade path miscalculation when the system reports its
version as a point release rather than the base version, which would
cause the installer to incorrectly skip or retry upgrade steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@ declare -f log_info &>/dev/null || log_info() { log_detail "$1"; }
 # ============================================================
 
 # Get current Ubuntu version as comparable number
-# e.g., 24.04 -> 2404, 25.10 -> 2510
+# e.g., 24.04 -> 2404, 25.10 -> 2510, 24.04.1 -> 2404
 # Returns: version number on stdout, or empty if not Ubuntu
 ubuntu_get_version_number() {
     local version_str
@@ -49,12 +49,15 @@ ubuntu_get_version_number() {
         return 1
     fi
 
-    # Convert "24.04" to "2404"
+    # Handle "24.04", "24.04.1", "25.10"
     local major minor
     major="${version_str%%.*}"
-    minor="${version_str#*.}"
+    # Remove everything after the second dot (if any) to get minor version
+    # "04.1" -> "04"
+    local remainder="${version_str#*.}"
+    minor="${remainder%%.*}"
 
-    # Pad minor to 2 digits
+    # Pad minor to 2 digits (e.g. 4 -> 04, 10 -> 10)
     printf "%d%02d" "$major" "$minor"
 }
